lesson: Minimum-jerk locked-lead catch reduces live Tkk; support can lead slightly, packet rematch must stay locked.

primary_channel: radial pressure p_l and Tkk burden
lesson: Wider edge reduces p_l and Tkk, but rapidly consumes packet safety margin.
Does not directly solve p_l; buys room to use stronger radial softening.
Small smoothing may help Tkk; did not materially solve p_l; excessive smoothing risks exposure.
Moving shell outward improves fractions but increases infrastructure burden; R1.75 was better compromise than R2.0.
Live packet pOmega is already tiny; global fit requires better basis, not urgent for passenger exposure.
Several localized time/edge modes work better than one smooth geometry-side lump.
Candidate is validated as V=10 tuned branch; safe at V=2/5/10; fails just beyond V=10 in tested branch.
